summary: Joel announces the Japanese translation of his book 'Joel on Software,' thanking translator Yasushi Aoki and publisher Ohmsha. He notes the book is now available in five languages and praises the translation quality, highlighting how closely the translator worked with him on cultural references and jokes. He shares a personal anecdote about his father's book being translated to Japanese when he was a child, and his attempt to decipher the family name in Japanese characters. A reader correction clarifies the actual Japanese pronunciation of his surname.
key_points: The Japanese edition of 'Joel on Software' joins English, Italian, Korean, and Chinese translations
High-quality translation required close collaboration to handle cultural references and inside jokes
Japanese book manufacturing quality surpassed the English original
Joel connects the experience to his father's academic work being translated to Japanese years earlier
money_quotes: I'm certain the Japanese translation will be excellent because Yasushi-san worked closely with me on many small points of translation and worked very hard to correctly translate all those weird cultural references and inside jokes I always put in my articles.
The quality of the physical book is much nicer than the English original (why do they have such nice paper in Japan?)
I was thrilled that our name in Japanese is usually pronounced sa-po-ra-sa-ki, which sounds like a cross between an alcoholic drink and a rad motorcycle.
key_insight: Good translation of technical writing requires close author-translator collaboration, especially when the writing is full of cultural references and humor.
